title: Step 3: To confirm a user removal, click .

sections.heading: Add Users to an Organization
sections.body:
> **Important:** 
>
> Atlas limits Atlas user membership to a maximum of 500
> Atlas users per organization.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo invite a user to your organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas users invite [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas users invite](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-users-invite).**Ui:**Atlas UITo invite users to an organization using the Atlas UI:* Click Invite Users.

* From the Add Users page, enter the new user's email
  address or Jira username in the combo box.

  After typing in the email address, either press
  Enter or click on the email address beneath the
  New User header under the combo box.

* Repeat for any additional users.By default, each user is given the [Organization Member](<Organization Member>)
role. To change or add additional roles for each user, click the
role dropdown menu, then select the checkboxes for each [organization role](organization-roles) you want the user to have in the organization.Click Add Users to Organization.When you invite an organization member to projects within the
organization, the user is automatically granted access to
those projects and doesn't receive any invitations. When you invite a
user to projects in an organization that the user doesn't belong to,
the user receives a single invitation to the organization, which
includes access to all of the projects that you grant them access to.
Invitations expire after 30 days.

sections.heading: View Active Users and Outgoing Invitations in an Organization
View Active Users
sections.body:
To list all users in your organization using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas organizations users list [options]

```To return the details for a user you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas users describe [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas organizations users list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-users-list) and
[atlas users describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-users-describe).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

sections.heading: View Pending User Invitations
sections.body:
To list all pending invitations to the organization you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas organizations invitations list [options]

```To return the details for one pending invitation to the organization you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas organizations invitations describe <invitationId> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas organizations invitations list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-invitations-list) and
[atlas organizations invitations describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-invitations-describe).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

sections.heading: Modify or Cancel a Pending User Invitation
sections.body:
To update one pending invitation to the organization you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas organizations invitations update [invitationId] [options]

```To delete one pending invitation to the organization you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas organizations invitations delete <invitationId> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas organizations invitations update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-invitations-update) and
[atlas organizations invitations delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-invitations-delete).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)**Ui:**Atlas UITo view users in an organization using the Atlas UI:This page lists:* Users who are members of your Atlas organization.

* Pending users who have not accepted the invitation to join
  the organization or its project within the organization.To cancel an invitation, click trash-alt to the right of
the pending user.

sections.heading: Edit User's Role in an Organization
sections.body:
